$comment: SMGP team-season history capsules, season 3 (SMGP-024). Base-universe history: the world's own arc, NOT the player career. DISPLAY-ONLY, never a fold input.

team.madonna.summary: Every dry Sunday read like a correction issued from the throne. The wettest calendar in memory asked the question the MADONNA 456 dislikes: not speed, but swimming, and at Brazil and in Belgium's flooded forest even the yellow-and-red queued in a backstreet car's spray, whatever the flag finally read. Then the road dried, and A. Senna restored the order with cold, wordless arithmetic, the PALM 190 V10 serene, a dry-day streak gathering through autumn. A. Asselin kept the door shut on the drowned and desperate. The house's verdict on the year: weather is temporary, the crown is not.
team.madonna.objective: Wait out the rain, win the dry, and remind the ladder the throne is not a raft.
team.madonna.definingEvent: The flooded Belgian afternoon the number one car queued in a LEVEL C machine's spray like everyone else.
team.madonna.carryForward: A dry-day streak to defend, and a paddock asking whether the rain told the truth or lied.

team.firenze.summary: The house of opera does not improvise, and the wettest year in memory was one long improvisation. F. Elssler read standing water as he reads everything, without sentiment, and banked his cold arithmetic while brasher men aquaplaned into the scenery; the ice travels well. The FIRENZE 99 V12 was less amused, its dizzy band a tightrope in the murk, and I. Germi's tight-round masterclasses had to be conducted with one ear on the clouds. The grandstands got fewer arias and more salvage. The wine-dark ledger survived the floods intact, which in this season counted as a command performance.
team.firenze.objective: Survive the improvisation with the ledger intact and the ice unmelted.
team.firenze.definingEvent: The ice travelling: Elssler's cold arithmetic banking while the brave aquaplaned into the scenery.
team.firenze.carryForward: A respectful new entry in the threat file: rain, and the men who read it cheapest.

team.millions.summary: Money bought the best weather men in the paddock and discovered it had purchased umbrellas, not climate. The MILLIONS 189 was magnificent on dry Fridays and merely expensive on wet Sundays, the DICK MD V10's headline top end muffled by standing water, N. Jones's three boasts per aria dissolving into the spray. G. Alberti, a champion, drove like a man who remembered an older war and banked the garage's self-respect. The wet-tyre truck was guarded like the gold it cost. The year's lesson, entered with distaste: some commodities refuse cornering, and rain is one.
team.millions.objective: Corner the weather market, and keep the gold dry enough to fight.
team.millions.definingEvent: The wet-tyre truck guarded like gold bullion, the season's truest picture of wealth.
team.millions.carryForward: A meteorology department on the payroll, and a grudge against the sky.

team.bestowal.summary: Chaos is the one currency the given house does not hold, and the wet year was a season of counterfeit. The BESTOWAL 167 posted the correct lap every wet Sunday and watched the brave cars post the fast one, the sealed VAPOR DN never once the issue, the wet manners correct rather than inspired, which is precisely the complaint. Blume's metronome cannot model standing water; Picos's warm precision came closest. The house hated the year with patrician discipline: no tantrum, just a filed note that the next rulebook should price weather out of reach.
team.bestowal.objective: Endure the counterfeit season and keep the correctness dry for when it counts.
team.bestowal.definingEvent: The wet Sundays the 167 posted the right lap and the brave cars posted the fast one.
team.bestowal.carryForward: A filed conviction that the next formula must price weather out of the argument.

team.blanche.summary: The white ledger's one untidy column is weather, and the rain came all season. J. Herbin's metronome kept honest time in the murk, P. White believed as instructed, and the BLANCHE 582 remained spotless, unbent, and slower than the men who read clouds for a living. The DELTA 103 V10's predictability is worth ten-tenths on a dry road, less on a river. The whitecoats logged every soaked Sunday as a stain that must never repeat, then did the most Blanche thing imaginable: hired a weather man, gave him a clean coat, and filed him under equipment.
team.blanche.objective: Stay spotless in the splash zone and file the wet season as a solvable stain.
team.blanche.definingEvent: The soaked Sundays logged as stains, and the weather man hired, coated, and filed under equipment.
team.blanche.carryForward: A cloud reader on the payroll, and a feud paused only because Tyrant was drowning too.

team.tyrant.summary: Rain is Armco you cannot see, and Tyrant has never feared Armco. The wet year suited the boot camp fine: M. Hamano read the flooded crossovers like his own pulse, G. Gould treated standing water as a dare with weather in it, and the TYRANT 548's late braking turned soaked braking zones into a fight. The LIZZIE 24 V8, crisp in the wet, made both men braver, which LEVEL B found unnecessary. The Blanche feud paused only because both garages were busy bailing. Upstairs they learned a new reason to check the mirrors: the gunmetal cars like it wet.
team.tyrant.objective: Turn the floods into contested ground and make LEVEL B fear the splash.
team.tyrant.definingEvent: Hamano reading the flooded crossovers like his own pulse while braver men than most lifted.
team.tyrant.carryForward: A reputation for wet menace, and a Blanche feud merely postponed, not settled.

team.losel.summary: The wind-tunnel house met the one variable it cannot graph, and the wet year graphed it anyway, in mud, on tyres, up every sidepod on the grid. E. Pacheco's cold equations held on dry road and dissolved off it, W. Dehehe's blue needle flickered for the first time in memory, and the LOSEL 125's beautiful consistency developed a second ledger column marked weather. The data room's verdict was professional humiliation: their model called the crossover one lap late; a flower at the back called it one early. The winter hire was inevitable. Losel bought itself a cloud.
team.losel.objective: Graph the ungraphable, and never lose a crossover to a gardener again.
team.losel.definingEvent: The model one lap late, the flower one lap early: the data room's professional humiliation.
team.losel.carryForward: A cloud on the payroll and a second column in the ledger marked weather.

team.may.summary: A sunshine team learned what a year without sun costs. G. Turner kept waving, because the wave is policy, but the MAY 555's trick is warm asphalt and there was barely any: the little green car spent the year huddled under awnings, blooming on the season's three dry afternoons and moping through the rest. The LORRY 32 V8's lazy torque proved the garage's umbrella, patient hands keeping it off the Armco while brighter machinery aquaplaned. The flowers at Azalea and Iris sent no condolences. Spring's ledger: survival, one perfect sun-baked Sunday, and a new appreciation for weather men.
team.may.objective: Survive the sunless year and treasure the three dry afternoons.
team.may.definingEvent: The one perfect Sunday the sun came out and the green car bloomed like a reminder.
team.may.carryForward: An umbrella found in the workhorse engine, and a weather man's number in the drawer.

team.bullets.summary: The backstreet car spent the summer leading the establishment through standing water, and the establishment is still towelling down. At Brazil the LEVEL A men squinted into the #17's spray all afternoon; in the flooded Belgian forest they queued in it, a day retold nightly on the tour. G. Ceara dragged a LEVEL C address deeper into the title argument than any backstreet car had been. The featherweight bones and the LIZZIE 24 V8's crisp honesty were built for this. The grandees' complaint says it all: a frozen formula plus a flooded calendar equals a championship money cannot steer.
team.bullets.objective: Turn the flooded calendar into the insurgency's coming-out season.
team.bullets.definingEvent: The half-flooded Belgium classic: the LEVEL A men queued in a LEVEL C car's spray, retold nightly on the tour.
team.bullets.carryForward: No longer a rumour but a championship argument, and the grandees officially out of laughter.

team.dardan.summary: You cannot protest the sky, and the clan spent the wet year discovering how much of its war was weather-dependent. The hearings gathered dust while the sister chassis made its coronation tour of the floods, a sight filed under things that will be answered. On track the DARDAN 700 came into its element: wetter walls, longer knife-fights, K. Alfven's ice-over-oxblood hands the clan's best ever in the murk. The VAPOR DNPQ V8's coarse, steady torque kept the spear straight where finer engines spun. A truce was never declared. It was merely rained off, and the difference matters to Dardan.
team.dardan.objective: Race the floods, bank the grudge, and keep the spear straight where finer engines spin.
team.dardan.definingEvent: The hearings rained off while the sister chassis took its coronation tour of the floods.
team.dardan.carryForward: A truce that was never declared, merely rained off, and a debt the clan still means to collect.

team.linden.summary: Old roots like rain. The LINDEN LN198 spent the wet year doing what it does in every weather: finishing, the LORRY 32 V8's lazy torque a gift to M. Moreau's patient hands, the harvest coming in while thirstier machines floated off. The season's true weight came at Portugal, when half a dozen cars gathered in one gravel trap and every man walked away: the stewards' bulletin proposing a standing pace-car law was shouted down within a fortnight, and Moreau, chief among the older heads, filed the proposal away. Some ideas are planted, not adopted; the tree knows how to wait.
team.linden.objective: Harvest the wet weather and plant the season's one important idea.
team.linden.definingEvent: Portugal: everyone walked away, the pace-car proposal was shouted down, and Moreau quietly filed it.
team.linden.carryForward: A written-down idea kept in the old root's pocket for a harder day.

team.minarae.summary: The on-ramp's canyon brawler brought a notebook to the flood. B. Miller, forged in the concrete canyons, reads standing water like a street sign, and his full-wet notebook, brim angle, throttle patience, where the rivers cross, became the grid's quietest rain masterclass, borrowed by half the paddock. J. Nono learned more in one wet year than three dry ones, the SEGA SG1000 V8 again proving itself the best first teacher, honest and undrownable. The 594 stayed the plainest car on the grid; in the rain, plain is a compliment the fast cars pay.
team.minarae.objective: Teach the grid how to swim and bill them in respect.
team.minarae.definingEvent: Miller's full-wet notebook: the canyon brawler's rain masterclass, borrowed by half the paddock.
team.minarae.carryForward: A notebook that outranks horsepower, and two drivers the wet made better.

team.rigel.summary: Rain levels what the ladder unlevels, and one grey afternoon the light came close. A wet qualifying session turned the 3000's old balance into the grid's most eloquent argument against horsepower, the lifers reading the rivers the way they read everything: from memory. The LORRY 32 V8's lazy traction kept the midnight-blue car hooked where the eager machinery spun, and the museum posted a grid slot unseen for seasons. Sunday returned the usual order, as Sunday does. The festival was held anyway, the good bottle opened, the ledger updated. The star, for one Saturday, burned at its old brightness.
team.rigel.objective: Catch the levelling rain and hold one Saturday at the old brightness.
team.rigel.definingEvent: The wet qualifying slot, posted from memory, the museum's closest touch of the light in seasons.
team.rigel.carryForward: Proof, bottled and ledgered, that the star's light is delayed, not dead.

team.comet.summary: Even the plume was damp this year. The wet year gave the COMET 323 what it craves: cold air and equalising chaos, and E. Tornio's silent hands produced the floor's finest party trick, a deluge flyer ahead of machinery with ten times the budget, twice. The LIZZIE 24 V8, crisp in the wet, sang through the spray until the reliability account came due, as it always does. The quarrel with Cool over who peaked hardest paused for weather, resumed over towels. Hope is a maintenance schedule, and rain, it turns out, is a lubricant the garage can afford.
team.comet.objective: Use the levelling rain to burn bright, and pay the reliability bill without complaint.
team.comet.definingEvent: The deluge qualifying flyers, two Saturdays of the old brightness in the spray.
team.comet.carryForward: A discovered fondness for wet weather and a quarrel resumed over towels.

team.orchis.summary: The flower that wilts in heat read the sky like scripture, and the wet year became the greenhouse's one summer. Mid-spring, with the sky merely threatening, C. Tegner made the call the paddock misread: wet rubber one lap before the heavens admitted anything, and the ORCHIS 056 inherited half the big board when the rain broke. The call made the frost gambler a cult overnight, the biggest swing in memory taken from a tyre choice rather than a pass. The MISFIRE 50 V8, breathing cool air, ran sweet. Midfield garages began hiring weather men; Orchis was ahead of the market.
team.orchis.objective: Read the sky one lap better than the paddock and cash the weather into points.
team.orchis.definingEvent: The one-lap-early tyre call: in while the sky was merely threatening, half the big board inherited when it broke.
team.orchis.carryForward: A cult hero on the payroll, and every rival garage now interviewing weather men.

team.zeroforce.summary: Attrition is the floor's only ally, and the wet year brought it by the truckload. When the road became a river, the men ahead fell off it, and P. Klinger's patience, the garage's one proven technology, brought the battered car home in places without precedent. The LORRY 32 V8's lazy torque was, for once, a fashion item. The wet-tyre rows passed over the garage; you cannot ration what was never allocated. The ledger reads: not last, more than once, in weather that made millionaires ordinary. The grey dignity went swimming and surfaced drier than most.
team.zeroforce.objective: Let the rain do the overtaking and be the thing still running at the flag.
team.zeroforce.definingEvent: Not last, more than once, in weather that made millionaires look ordinary.
team.zeroforce.carryForward: A season of floods banked as proof: the floor can swim.

team.joke.summary: The marine engine finally explained itself. The POND V8 had been the paddock's quiet punchline for years, and then the wettest calendar in memory arrived, and the punchline changed owners: waterproof electrics, oceanic tolerance, and a delivery impossible to drown made the #16 the one car still running when the road became a river. L. Dufay produced his finest theatre in the deluges, waving at drowning grandees as he passed. The sandbagging continued on dry Saturdays, for tradition. The grandees' complaint was music: a championship money cannot steer, clowned by a yacht.
team.joke.objective: Cash the waterproof inheritance and clown the drowning establishment.
team.joke.definingEvent: The deluge afternoons the #16 was still running when boats would have been the sensible choice.
team.joke.carryForward: A punchline that now cuts both ways, and grandees who check the sky for yachts.

team.lares.summary: Patience, the paddock finally admitted, is a weapon. The flooded calendar was the harvest P. Arai had spent a career planting: no heroics at all, just the stout LARES 92 finishing everything, the RAM V12's torque walking the hearth-red car through standing water while the brave spun off around it. The standings crept upward one sodden afternoon at a time, and by autumn the gentleman was having the best year of his life without once raising his voice. The lamp stayed lit, the door stayed open, and the guardian's record grew as it grows: quietly, past everyone who hurried.
team.lares.objective: Plant patience all spring and harvest the flooded autumn.
team.lares.definingEvent: The sodden-season harvest: the politest man in the paddock quietly having the best year of his life.
team.lares.carryForward: A reputation weaponised: the paddock now calls patience a weapon and means it.

team.feet.summary: Floods are just overtime for boots. The wet year turned every Sunday into the afternoons the grafters train for: slippery, unfair, and priced in effort rather than horsepower. J. Rampal, who drives like the seat keeps his lights on, was magnificent in the murk, dragging the FEET 13 through standing water past drivers who had never once been cold. The YOUGEN V10's unstressed thirst suited the attrition perfectly; Old Boots kept going while the pretty machines floated home. No trophy came of it; none ever does. What came was the paddock's mechanics, quietly watching the battered blue car and nodding.
team.feet.objective: Turn the floods into overtime and out-last the pretty machinery.
team.feet.definingEvent: Rampal's murk masterclasses, dragging Old Boots past drivers who had never once been cold.
team.feet.carryForward: The paddock's mechanics nodding again, and a reputation for waterproof stubbornness.

team.serga.summary: Mountain weather recognises mountain men. The wet year felt, to the Andorran who welded the SERGA 1000 himself, less like crisis than home fixture: the SC3000 F12 is a mountain engine, cold until warmed, then broad and eager, and Sambena drove the floods like a man who learned on weather, not asphalt. The oiling kept its opinions; the spares shelf stayed thin; the results, for once, did not. On the season's worst afternoon the privateer came home ahead of half the establishment; the roar was worth more than the points, which were banked.
team.serga.objective: Make the floods a home fixture and bank the points pride cannot spend.
team.serga.definingEvent: The season's worst afternoon, the grey car home ahead of half the establishment.
team.serga.carryForward: A thin shelf and a fattening ledger of respect, the only currency the garage mints.

team.cool.summary: It rained on the deckchairs and the postcard wrote itself. At his home forest, drowning in the deluge, A. Delvaux hung on for a points finish, and the place roared as if a title had been settled, which at the cheap end is exactly what had happened. The CORSE V8's electrics sulked on schedule, then, loved back to life by men in no hurry, ran sweetly through the worst of it. The Comet quarrel paused for weather, both crews sharing an awning like castaways. The verdict, from a damp deckchair: staying is winning; dry socks are for crown teams.
team.cool.objective: Hang on in the home deluge and give the soaked grandstand its sunset.
team.cool.definingEvent: Belgium: Delvaux's points finish in the forest rain, roared home like a settled title.
team.cool.carryForward: A home hero made of drizzle, and an awning truce with the comet men.

team.moon.summary: The far side gets weather last and keeps it longest, and the wet year lingered over the silver car. K. Yepes waved at the spray, circled the floods with a body's orbital patience, and finished more often than the eager: the RAM V12's torque does not aquaplane, it merely slows, which this season was a strategy. The moonshots stayed unfired; you do not gamble an irreplaceable car on an invisible road. The logbook's line for the year: present at more flags than fourteen other garages. From down here, the rain was just more light to borrow.
team.moon.objective: Slow down less than the eager and be present at every flag.
team.moon.definingEvent: Present at more flags than fourteen other garages, the logbook's quietest triumph.
team.moon.carryForward: Another year of borrowed light banked, and the orbit measurably higher.

team.iris.summary: The Violet Flame is the one grandee who grins when the clouds gather. B. Salgado spent the wet year threading the IRIS 717 through standing water where machinery tiptoed, the white gloves given something worth gripping, the wet-podium record becoming the public programme. The PRISM 90 V10's band is a tightrope in the murk; Salgado walked it like a man rehearsed on worse. He was the only LEVEL A answer to the insurgent's spray, the season's establishment resistance. The crown stayed where it was, dry-day property. The violet treasury grew the way the rain fell: all season.
team.iris.objective: Be the establishment's answer to the spray and chase the wet-podium record.
team.iris.definingEvent: The standing-water afternoons the purple car danced where its neighbours tiptoed.
team.iris.carryForward: A wet-weather crown of his own making, and the sister reminded which jewel the rain prefers.

team.azalea.summary: The frost flower blooms when the lights burn brightest, and the wet year was one long grey floodlight. On the season's marquee afternoon the AZALEA 808 opened while the grandees wilted, the BLOOM 88 V8 building from held breath to full song as the day reached its decisive hour. M. Larssen drove standing water like a man who considers grip a rumour started by slower hands. The sister war tilted: Iris owns the spray, the paddock admitted, but Azalea owns the afternoons that matter. The cabinet gained one more entry the whole grid can date.
team.azalea.objective: Bloom on the wet season's one marquee afternoon and let the sisters argue the rest.
team.azalea.definingEvent: The grey marquee afternoon the bloom opened while the grandees wilted.
team.azalea.carryForward: The family ledger tilted: the spray has an owner, but the decisive days have another.
